data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-cfedee8e">
    <title>escalation: wire ladder into executor closure; drop tier→profile mutation</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The escalation ladder (escalation.Ladder.Next) was added in S10_2 but has no production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. Each bead currently gets exactly one singleTierAttempt without any rung escalation on substantive failure. The existing tier→profile mutation in the loop is an ad-hoc workaround that should be replaced by the ladder.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: singleTierAttempt is called exactly once per bead iteration; there is no loop over ladder rungs.
- cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:30: Ladder.Next is defined but has no call site in execute_bead_loop.go or any other production file (confirmed by dead-code audit ddx-09d2990c).
- The existing tier→profile mutation (a manual MinPower bump) in the executor closure is the only escalation behavior; it is not driven by the ladder contract.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the single singleTierAttempt call in execute_bead_loop.go with a real loop that:
  1. Calls singleTierAttempt with current rung's MinPower.
  2. On substantive failure (non-park, non-budget-exceeded), calls ladder.Next to get next rung.
  3. Terminates when ladder.Next returns no further rungs or a stop condition triggers.
- Remove the existing tier→profile mutation; ladder.Next drives all MinPower escalation.
- Pass Role='implementer' and CorrelationID='<bead_id>:<attempt_id>' on every ExecuteRequest.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the ladder definition or rung configuration (S10_2 owns that).
- Cost-cap relocation (belongs to C9 / ddx-9228a484).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. execute_bead_loop.go uses escalation.Ladder.Next on substantive failure (confirmed call site in production code, not just defined).
2. Existing tier→profile mutation removed from execute_bead_loop.go.
3. Every ExecuteRequest carries Role='implementer' and CorrelationID='<bead_id>:<attempt_id>'.
4. TestEscalationLadder_WiredIntoExecutor verifies ladder.Next is called when singleTierAttempt returns a substantive failure.
5. TestEscalationLadder_StopsAtFinalRung verifies loop terminates when ladder.Next exhausts rungs.
6. Tests in cli/internal/agent/zero_config_escalation_integration_test.go (or new test file) exercise actual escalation, not just primitives.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
PENDING-SUPERSEDE: C9 (ddx-9228a484) explicitly absorbs this bead's scope (MinPower-bump-per-rung ladder logic). Close as superseded when C9 lands. Do NOT claim independently — claim work goes into C9.
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:routing, kind:refactor</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="0944e5f022fcdf779252c3a18817a3993815144e" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260505T044847-0ae55cdf"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T044847-0ae55cdf/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T044847-0ae55cdf/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T044847-0ae55cdf/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T044847-0ae55cdf/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T044847-0ae55cdf/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `75b6160a` (`fix: wire escalation ladder into worker executor [ddx-cfedee8e]`).

What changed:
- Wired `escalation.Ladder.Next` into the executor path in [`cli/internal/server/workers.go`](./cli/internal/server/workers.go).
- Removed the old tier→profile mutation from that path.
- Propagated `Role="implementer"` and `CorrelationID="<bead_id>:<attempt_id>"` through the agent request plumbing in:
  - [`cli/internal/agent/types.go`](./cli/internal/agent/types.go)
  - [`cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`](./cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go)
  - [`cli/internal/agent/run_internal.go`](./cli/internal/agent/run_internal.go)
  - [`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go`](./cli/internal/agent/service_run.go)
  - [`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go`](./cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go)
  - [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`](./cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go)
- Added ladder wiring tests in [`cli/internal/server/workers_escalation_test.go`](./cli/internal/server/workers_escalation_test.go).
- Updated the server log-format assertion in [`cli/internal/server/workers_test.go`](./cli/internal/server/workers_test.go).
- Added a passthrough test for the new request fields in [`cli/internal/agent/passthrough_envelope_test.go`](./cli/internal/agent/passthrough_envelope_test.go).

Verification:
- `go test ./internal/agent/...`
- `go test ./internal/server/...`
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`

Working tree is clean.
